Based on the retrieved evidence, bortozmib is definitively classified as a proteasome inhibitor because it irreversibly binds to the chymotrypsin-like subunit of the 26S proteasome, blocking protein degradation and promoting cancer cell death through apoptosis. \boxed{A}
